phases.name: Core Foundation
phases.description: Binary starts, database works, providers connect, sessions stream. The minimum viable agent platform.

phases.name: Agent Tools & Observability
phases.description: Agents can execute tools server-side. Traces and journey view provide visibility into agent behavior.

phases.name: Frontend
phases.description: Browser UI for creating sessions, chatting with agents, and observing their work.

phases.name: Kanban
phases.description: Kanban boards with task management and lane automation that triggers specialist sessions.

phases.name: Extended Features
phases.description: Agent-to-agent communication, notes, artifacts, codebase management, and production readiness.

phases.name: Native Tool Loop
phases.description: Complete the native Anthropic tool-execution loop. Prerequisite for all wrapped-CLI work because journey and trace shapes depend on tools actually executing and being recorded. Anthropic API in native mode must keep its current behavior and gain a real tool-use loop.

phases.name: Multi-Runtime Foundation
phases.description: Schema, model, and trait surface changes to carry runtime/mode on a session and a per-Runtime-Tool provider config. No user-facing surface change yet. The Provider table widens to a discriminated union (HTTP vs CLI); the CodingAgent trait gains a per-turn context; runtime x mode compatibility is enforced at session creation.

phases.name: Claude Code CLI Wrapped Mode
phases.description: First CLI runtime target. Per-turn subprocess runner, fake-CLI test harness, Claude Code stream-json parser, PermissionMapper, CLI-native resume id persistence with message-history replay fallback, CLI -> journey translation, child-process reaping on crash, workspace-scoped CLI session validation, and an end-to-end CliCodingAgent conformance test. Anthropic API in native mode stays green throughout.

phases.name: Multi-Runtime User Surface
phases.description: Settings, session creation, kanban, and A2A surfaces that let a user pick a Runtime Tool explicitly. The silent first-provider fallback is replaced with explicit Runtime Tool selection or a configured default.

phases.name: Additional CLI Runtimes
phases.description: Codex and OpenCode adapters, built on the shared CliRunner, parser framework, PermissionMapper, fake-CLI harness, and conformance suite from Phase 8. Each adapter must pass the conformance suite to ship. Real CLI smoke tests are manual.

phases.name: Attended Mode (Deferred)
phases.description: Attended mode is a separate Terminal abstraction (not a CodingAgent impl). Deferred until Phase 8 wrapped mode is stable and the lifecycle split (Weave drives the CLI vs. the user drives the CLI) is justified by real usage.

features.behavior: Binary compiles and starts. CLI accepts --host, --port, --db-path, --allow-remote flags via clap. Tracing subscriber initializes with RUST_LOG env filter. Server binds and listens. GET /api/health returns {\\\"status\\\": \\\"ok\\\", \\\"version\\\": \\\"...\\\", \\\"uptime_seconds\\\": N}. Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT.
features.verification: cargo build -p weave-server && cargo run -p weave-server -- --port 19876 &; sleep 2; curl -sf http://localhost:19876/api/health | jq -e '.status == \\\"ok\\\"'; kill %1
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: SQLite opens with WAL mode, foreign keys ON, 5s busy timeout. Versioned migrations run on startup via include_str! embedded SQL files. Schema creates all 11 tables: workspaces, sessions, messages, providers, codebases, worktrees, boards, columns, tasks, traces, file_changes. Indexes on foreign keys and status columns.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_db_init && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_migrations_idempotent
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: Shared error types: AppError enum (NotFound, Validation, Provider, Database, Internal) with thiserror. ProviderError enum (AuthFailed, RateLimited, ModelNotFound, Unreachable, StreamInterrupted). AppError implements IntoResponse mapping errors to HTTP status codes + consistent JSON envelope {\\\"error\\\": {\\\"code\\\": \\\"...\\\", \\\"message\\\": \\\"...\\\"}}.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_error_response_format && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_error_status_codes
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: WorkspaceStore with CRUD operations. Default workspace \\\"default\\\" created on first startup. API: GET /api/workspaces (paginated), POST /api/workspaces (name validation 1-100 chars), GET /api/workspaces/:id, PATCH /api/workspaces/:id, DELETE /api/workspaces/:id (cascade deletes). All responses use consistent JSON envelope.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_workspace_crud && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_workspace_default_seed
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: CodingAgent trait defined: provider_type(), display_name(), list_models(), send_message(MessageRequest) -> Stream<StreamEvent>, health_check(). StreamEvent enum: TextDelta, ToolUseStart, ToolUseDelta, ToolResult, Thinking, Done(StopReason), Error. StopReason enum: EndTurn, MaxTokens, ToolUse, Cancelled. Supporting types: MessageRequest, Message, Role, Content, ContentBlock, ToolDefinition, ModelInfo, ProviderHealth. All types are Send + Sync.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_stream_event_variants && cargo build -p weave-server (trait compiles)
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: AnthropicAgent implements CodingAgent. Sends POST to {base_url}/v1/messages with stream:true. Parses Anthropic SSE events (message_start, content_block_start, content_block_delta, content_block_stop, message_delta, message_stop) into StreamEvent items. Handles errors: 401->AuthFailed, 429->RateLimited (with Retry-After), 404->ModelNotFound, 500/529->retryable. list_models() returns hardcoded Claude model list. health_check() sends minimal request to verify credentials.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_anthropic_sse_parsing && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_anthropic_error_mapping
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: ProviderStore persists providers to SQLite. ProviderRegistry holds HashMap<String, Arc<dyn CodingAgent>>, loads from DB on startup. Runtime CRUD: add_provider (validates config, creates agent, inserts DB), remove_provider (fails if active sessions reference it), get_agent by ID. API: GET /api/providers (strips api_key from response), POST /api/providers, DELETE /api/providers/:id, GET /api/providers/:id/models.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_provider_crud && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_provider_api_key_stripped
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: SessionStore persists sessions and messages. Session state machine: connecting -> ready -> completed/cancelled/error. API: POST /api/workspaces/:wid/sessions (create with provider_id, optional specialist_id/model/parent_session_id), GET /api/workspaces/:wid/sessions (paginated), GET /api/sessions/:id, DELETE /api/sessions/:id. Messages stored as immutable JSON content blocks. GET /api/sessions/:sid/history with cursor-based pagination.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_session_lifecycle && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_session_state_transitions && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_message_pagination
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: SessionService orchestrates session lifecycle. send_prompt: validates session status, saves user message, loads specialist system prompt (if set), builds message history, resolves tool set (initially empty), calls agent.send_message, spawns async task that streams events -> broadcasts to SSE channel -> accumulates response -> saves assistant message -> updates session status. POST /api/sessions/:sid/prompt returns message_id immediately. POST /api/sessions/:sid/cancel aborts the agent stream.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_send_prompt_flow && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_cancel_session
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: SSE infrastructure: SseManager with per-entity broadcast channels (tokio::broadcast). EventBuffer with ring buffer (100 events per entity). GET /api/sessions/:sid/stream returns SSE stream with connected, text_delta, tool_use_start, tool_use_delta, tool_result, thinking, done, error, heartbeat (15s) events. Supports Last-Event-ID header for reconnection. Backpressure: lagged subscriber receives gap event. SessionService broadcasts all agent events through this layer.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_broadcast_and_subscribe && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_event_buffer_replay && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_sse_broadcast_on_prompt
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: SpecialistLoader scans resources/specialists/*.md, parses YAML frontmatter (name, description, model, tool_profile, tags), extracts markdown body as system prompt. Loads into HashMap<String, Specialist> at startup. Logs count of loaded specialists and skipped files. Injects system prompt into session's first message when specialist_id is set. Handles: malformed YAML (skip + warn), missing required fields (skip + warn), invalid UTF-8 (skip + warn).
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_specialist_loading && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_specialist_frontmatter_parse && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_specialist_malformed_yaml
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: ToolRegistry holds HashMap<String, Arc<dyn ToolExecutor>> and profiles HashMap<String, Vec<String>>. ToolExecutor trait: name(), input_schema(), execute(input, context) -> ToolContext contains session_id, cwd, codebase_root, trace_collector. ToolResult: {success, data, error}. Five profiles defined: full (all tools), implementation (fs+shell+git+task), review (fs-read+git+task+artifacts), planning (task+kanban+notes), reporting (task-read+notes+artifacts). SessionService filters tools by specialist's profile when building MessageRequest.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_tool_profile_filtering && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_send_prompt_invalid_tool_profile
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: Filesystem tools: fs_read (read file content), fs_write (write/overwrite, creates parent dirs), fs_edit (search/replace patch, unique match required), fs_search (regex search with glob filter), fs_list (directory listing, recursive option). All paths must be absolute. Write operations restricted to session's codebase root (symlink-aware canonicalization, no .. traversal). Control-plane paths (.git/, weave.db, Cargo.toml, resources/specialists/, resources/migrations/, .claude/, CLAUDE.md, DECISIONS.md, PROGRESS.md, feature_list.json, init.sh, justfile) are read-only. Tool results include success flag, bytes written/read, error messages with fix instructions.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_file_write_read && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_file_path_containment && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_control_plane_protection
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: Shell tool: shell_exec with command, optional cwd, optional timeout_ms (default 30000). Runs via tokio::process::Command. Captures stdout + stderr + exit code. Timeout kills the process. Cwd defaults to session's working directory. Output truncated at 100KB. Records trace event with duration.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_shell_exec_basic && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_shell_exec_timeout && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_shell_exec_cwd
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: Git tools: git_status (branch, staged/unstaged/untracked), git_diff (unified diff, truncated 50KB, optional staged/file filter), git_log (recent commits with hash+message, default 10), git_commit (message, optional stage_all, validates real user.name/user.email \\u2014 rejects placeholder/test identities). All tools take cwd parameter, default to session working directory.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_git_status && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_git_commit_validation
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: Task context tools: get_task (current task details with title, description, acceptance criteria), list_tasks (filter by status/column_id), update_task_status (in_progress, review_required, completed, needs_fix, blocked), update_task_fields (completion_summary, verification_report). These tools are the bridge between kanban tasks and agent sessions \\u2014 agents know what they're working on.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_get_task_context && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_update_task_status
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: TraceCollector records events during agent execution. Traces persisted to SQLite. Event types: tool_call (name, input, output, duration_ms), file_change (path, action: read/write/create/delete), decision (extracted from Thinking blocks), error (message, recovery), milestone, review. GET /api/sessions/:sid/trace returns full trace ordered by timestamp. GET /api/sessions/:sid/trace/journey returns filtered Decision+Milestone+Review events. GET /api/sessions/:sid/trace/files returns deduplicated file change list.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_trace_collection && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_journey_summary && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_file_change_summary
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: Session resume: POST /api/workspaces/:wid/sessions with parent_session_id copies all messages from parent session into new session. New session starts with full conversation history as context. SessionService handles the message copying within a transaction. Supports chaining (grandparent sessions resolved recursively up to depth 5).
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_session_resume && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_session_resume_chain
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: React frontend scaffolding: Vite + React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + TanStack Query + React Router. API wrapper (src/lib/api.ts) with fetch, consistent error handling, base URL config. Types file (src/lib/types.ts) matching backend API contracts. Vite dev server proxies /api to backend. Builds with zero TypeScript errors.

features.behavior: Home page: workspace list with create/rename/delete. Workspace detail page: session list with status badges, create session button (pick provider + optional specialist + optional model). Settings page: provider list, add provider form (type, name, base_url, api_key, default_model), delete provider. All data via TanStack Query with loading skeletons and error banners.

features.behavior: Session chat view: MessageList renders user + assistant messages with MarkdownRenderer. ToolCallBlock (expandable) shows tool name, input JSON, output. StreamingIndicator shows agent is thinking. MessageInput with auto-resize textarea + send button. useSession hook: TanStack Query for initial data, EventSource for SSE stream, text_delta appends to local buffer, done invalidates history query. Auto-scroll to latest message.

features.behavior: Journey sidebar (collapsible): JourneyTimeline shows Decision + Error events in chronological order with timestamps. DecisionNode expandable to show full summary. FileChangesList shows deduplicated files touched in session with action badges (read/write/create/delete). Fetches from /api/sessions/:sid/trace/journey and /api/sessions/:sid/trace/files.
features.verification: cd web && bun run test -- --run journey-view
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: Frontend served from Rust binary. crates/weave-server/build.rs runs `bunx vite build` in ../../web at compile time (path resolved via env!(\\\"CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR\\\"), CWD-independent) and watches web/src, web/index.html, web/package.json, web/vite.config.ts, web/tsconfig.json, and web/bun.lock for changes. The build script invokes vite directly rather than `bun run build` to skip the pre-existing `tsc -b` type-check (the bundle is what gets embedded; full type-check runs in `just lint`/`just test`). Runtime: tower-http ServeDir serves web/dist with ServeFile(index.html) chained via ServeDir::fallback for SPA fallback (preserves ServeFile's 200 status, unlike not_found_service which would force 404); mounted as .fallback_service(spa_service()) at the end of api::router so /api/* routes still match first. Dev mode unchanged: Vite dev server with /api proxy to backend, no build.rs involvement. Tests: tower::ServiceExt::oneshot asserts GET / serves index.html, GET /sessions/abc-123 and GET /favicon.ico fall back to index.html with 200, GET /assets/<hashed>.js returns the JS bytes, and GET /api/health still returns the API route's response.
features.verification: cargo build -p weave-server && ./target/debug/weave-server --port 19876 &; sleep 2; curl -sf http://localhost:19876/ | grep -q 'root'; kill %1
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: KanbanStore: CRUD for boards, columns, tasks. Board belongs to workspace. Columns have position ordering, optional specialist_id binding, auto_trigger boolean. Tasks have position within column, status (active/done/archived), optional session_id. API: POST /api/workspaces/:wid/boards (with optional columns array for template), GET /api/workspaces/:wid/boards, GET /api/boards/:id (includes columns + tasks), PATCH /api/boards/:id, POST /api/boards/:bid/columns, PATCH /api/columns/:cid, POST /api/boards/:bid/cards, PATCH /api/tasks/:tid, DELETE /api/tasks/:tid.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_kanban_crud && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_kanban_column_ordering && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_kanban_task_position
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: KanbanService lane automation: when task moves to column with auto_trigger=true AND specialist_id set, automatically creates a session with that specialist's system prompt, associates session with task, sends initial prompt \\\"Process task: {title}\\\\n\\\\{description}\\\", broadcasts session_started event. KanbanSSE: GET /api/boards/:bid/stream with events task_created, task_moved, task_updated, task_deleted, column_added, session_started, heartbeat.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_lane_automation && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_kanban_sse_events
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: Kanban frontend: BoardContainer with horizontal scroll, Column with ColumnHeader (name, specialist badge, auto-trigger toggle), CardList with drag-and-drop via @dnd-kit/core, Card with title + status badge + agent indicator. AddColumnButton, AddCardButton. useKanban hook with TanStack Query + SSE for real-time updates (task_moved patches cache in-place, session_started invalidates board). TaskDetailPanel slide-over on card click.

features.behavior: Default board template: POST /api/workspaces/:wid/boards with columns=[Backlog(backlog-refiner,auto), To Do(todo-orchestrator,auto), In Progress(dev-crafter,auto), Review(review-guard,auto), Done(done-reporter,off)]. Built-in specialist markdown files in resources/specialists/: backlog-refiner.md, todo-orchestrator.md, dev-crafter.md, review-guard.md, done-reporter.md with appropriate tool_profile settings.

features.behavior: Kanban tools for agents: get_board (full board state with columns and cards), move_card (enforces transition gates \\u2014 required artifacts, required fields, description frozen from dev stage), create_card, search_cards (by query, labels, assignee). These tools let agents interact with the kanban board autonomously.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_kanban_tools && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_move_card_transition_gates
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol server endpoints: implements Google A2A v1.0 REST binding core subset. Server exposes an Agent Card at GET /.well-known/agent.json describing its capabilities (built from loaded specialists as skills). Agent endpoints: POST /api/a2a/messages (SendMessage — creates or continues a Weave session, sends prompt, returns Task), GET /api/a2a/tasks/{id} (GetTask — session status, optional history and artifacts), POST /api/a2a/tasks/{id}/cancel (CancelTask — idempotent session cancel), GET /api/a2a/tasks/{id}/subscribe (SubscribeToTask — SSE stream of TaskStatusUpdate events via futures::stream::unfold). Sessions are A2A targets (A2A task ID = session UUID). context_id on sessions links multiple sessions into one logical A2A task. Auth via Bearer token checked against WEAVE_A2A_TOKEN env var; auth disabled when not set (localhost dev).
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_a2a
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: Note/document tools for agents: create_note (title, content, type: spec/task/general -> returns note_id), read_note, list_notes (filter by type), set_note_content (replace), append_to_note. Notes stored in SQLite (notes table). Enables context persistence between agent sessions \\u2014 agents can write specs, read previous analysis, maintain shared state.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_note_crud && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_note_append

features.behavior: Artifact tools: request_artifact (task_id, type: screenshot/test_results/code_diff/logs, optional agent_id -> request_id), provide_artifact (task_id, type, content -> artifact_id), list_artifacts (task_id). Artifacts stored in SQLite (artifacts table). Agents can attach evidence to tasks \\u2014 required for kanban transition gates (review columns can require specific artifact types before allowing card movement).

features.behavior: CodebaseStore + API: register git repos as codebases within a workspace (path, optional branch, label). Unique index on (workspace_id, path). Sessions can be associated with a codebase via cwd matching. API: GET /api/workspaces/:wid/codebases, POST /api/workspaces/:wid/codebases, GET /api/codebases/:id (includes git status: current branch, dirty files, last 5 commits), DELETE /api/codebases/:id. Codebase view in frontend.

features.behavior: Enhanced health check: GET /api/health returns version, uptime_seconds, provider count (total/healthy/unhealthy), active_sessions count, database size_bytes, wal_checkpoint_pending status. Provider health_check_all runs in parallel. Useful for monitoring and debugging.

features.behavior: Graceful shutdown: on SIGTERM/SIGINT, stop accepting new connections, wait for in-flight requests (30s timeout), cancel all active agent sessions (abort provider streams), flush SQLite WAL checkpoint, log shutdown sequence. tokio::select! on signal and server handle. Active SSE clients receive disconnect event.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_graceful_shutdown
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; 605 Rust tests + 83 frontend tests pass (12 new: test_sse_wire_event_shutdown_type, test_broadcast_shutdown_fans_out_to_all_subscribers, test_broadcast_shutdown_no_subscribers_is_noop, test_active_sessions_cancel_all_cancels_every_token, test_active_sessions_cancel_all_empty_is_zero, test_active_sessions_len_tracks_inserts_and_removes, test_db_checkpoint_runs_and_returns_structured_result, test_reap_orphans_marks_non_terminal_sessions_as_error, test_reap_orphans_empty_database_is_noop, test_reap_orphans_idempotent, test_graceful_shutdown, test_shutdown_signal_with_cap_fires_token_on_timeout). Verification command cargo test -p weave-server -- test_graceful_shutdown exits 0. Smoke test reaped 12 orphan sessions from prior run and observed SIGTERM → cleanup → 'Server shut down gracefully' in the log.

features.behavior: Configuration: environment variables for all config options (WEAVE_HOST, WEAVE_PORT, WEAVE_DB_PATH, WEAVE_SPECIALISTS_DIR, WEAVE_ALLOW_REMOTE). CLI args take precedence over env vars, env vars take precedence over defaults. Optional TOML config file support (--config flag). Config validation on startup with clear error messages.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_config_env_vars && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_config_cli_precedence

features.behavior: Re-implement session chat streaming for smooth UX. (1) New SSE event `message_persisted` carrying the persisted row id, role, content, stop_reason, created_at is broadcast AFTER MessageStore::create returns and BEFORE the terminal `done` event. (2) On cancel and on streaming error, the partial streamed text is persisted with stop_reason=cancelled|error encoded in the existing messages.metadata column. (3) Frontend useSession is a useReducer with a discriminated Action union; content-equality dedup in LiveAssistantMessage is replaced with id-based handoff (`liveBubbleId !== persistedTurnId`). (4) dead state removed (thinkingChunks). (5) On user scroll-up while streaming, a 'jump to latest' pill appears. (6) Initial loading renders a 3-row skeleton, not a centered spinner. (7) Thinking blocks render inline and collapsible.
features.verification: cd web && bun run test && cargo test -p weave-server --lib
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass; verification command exits 0.

features.behavior: Complete the native Anthropic tool-execution loop in `AnthropicAgent::send_message`. The provider advertises Weave tools via MessageRequest.tools; on a `content_block_start` of type `tool_use` the agent collects the streamed `input_json_delta` events into a serde_json::Value, runs the named tool through ToolRegistry subject to the session's profile, appends a `tool_result` content block carrying the tool_use_id and the serialized result, and continues the model call. The `tool_use` `stop_reason` is the loop driver; the loop terminates on `end_turn` / `max_tokens` / `cancelled` / loop-limit-exceeded. Reuses TraceCollector to record tool_call events with name, sanitized input, output, and duration_ms. Handles: malformed input JSON, unknown tool name, tool input that fails JSON-schema validation, tool execution error (recorded in the tool_result and the loop continues), cancel mid-loop (the in-flight tool future is aborted, partial assistant text is persisted with stop_reason=cancelled), and a per-turn loop limit (default 8 iterations, configurable per session) that turns into stop_reason=error after a final `Sorry, too many tool calls` assistant message. Existing Anthropic API behavior is preserved: a model that does NOT call a tool is still a one-shot stream ending in `end_turn`.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_native_tool_loop_basic && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_native_tool_loop_unknown_tool && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_native_tool_loop_validation_error && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_native_tool_loop_exec_error && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_native_tool_loop_limit && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_native_tool_loop_cancellation && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_native_tool_loop_no_tool_passes_through
features.evidence: All 7 spec tests pass (cargo test -p weave-server test_native_tool_loop: 7 passed, 604 filtered out). Full ./init.sh green: cargo clippy clean, cargo fmt --check clean, 611 Rust tests + 83 frontend tests pass, cargo build succeeds, smoke test (GET /api/health + GET /) returns 200. Implementation adds a nested agent_loop in service/sessions.rs that drives the model <-> tool-execution loop (MAX_TOOL_ITERATIONS=8, TOOL_EXECUTION_TIMEOUT=30s), with StopReason::LoopLimit and ToolOutcome enum handling all five error paths (unknown tool, validation failure, tool error, cancel-mid-loop, loop-cap). Tool inputs are sanitized (whitespace trim) and validated against the tool's JSON Schema. ToolResult content blocks now carry is_error to the wire. build_message_metadata includes tool_calls summary and stop_reason in metadata JSON when any tool was called. A2A map_sse_to_a2a maps LoopLimit to TaskStatus::Failed. TraceCollector made Clone for the tool context. The Anthropic streaming EventConverter was changed to defer ToolUseStart emission until ContentBlockStop so streamed input_json_delta is assembled into a serde_json::Value before the tool is invoked.

features.behavior: Add three columns to `sessions`: `runtime_kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'anthropic-api'` (one of `anthropic-api` | `openai-api` | `openai-compatible` | `claude-code` | `codex` | `opencode`), `mode TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'native'` (one of `native` | `wrapped` | `attended`), and `runtime_metadata_json TEXT` (nullable, JSON-encoded per-runtime state including the CLI-native session id for resume). Migration is a new SQL file under `resources/migrations/` applied in order; existing rows backfill to `runtime_kind='anthropic-api', mode='native', runtime_metadata_json=NULL`. `SessionStore::create` and `update` accept and persist these fields; `get` and `list` return them. `MessageStore` and the SSE `done` event include the runtime_kind and mode in their payload. The `parent_session_id` resume chain (feat-018) inherits the parent session's runtime/mode by default but accepts an override on the new session row. Schema change is additive; no column renames.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_session_runtime_kind_migration && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_session_runtime_metadata_roundtrip && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_session_runtime_default_backfill

features.behavior: Widen the Provider table to a discriminated union. Add a `kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'http'` column to `providers` (`http` | `cli`). HTTP rows carry the existing `base_url`, `api_key`, `default_model` (unchanged). CLI rows carry a new `binary_path TEXT`, `args_json TEXT`, `env_json TEXT`, `default_model TEXT`, and `permission_mode TEXT` (one of `accept-edits` | `default` | `plan` | `bypass-permissions` | CLI-specific strings). Both variants keep `name` and `default_model`. Migration is additive; existing rows backfill to `kind='http'`. `POST /api/providers` accepts the `kind` discriminator and validates the right shape (http requires base_url + api_key; cli requires binary_path; both require name + default_model). `GET /api/providers` and `GET /api/providers/:id` return `kind` and the kind-specific config. `add_provider` in ProviderRegistry builds the matching `CodingAgent` (HTTP → `AnthropicAgent`-style; CLI → `CliCodingAgent` placeholder until feat-051). `api_key` is stripped from responses as today. The `/api/providers/:id/models` endpoint is still HTTP-only in this feature; the CLI model cache is feat-042.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_provider_kind_http_crud && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_provider_kind_cli_crud && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_provider_kind_validation && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_provider_api_key_stripped_across_kinds && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_provider_migration_backfills_http

features.behavior: Enforce the Runtime Tool x mode compatibility matrix from §4 of the strategy at session creation and at kanban / A2A binding time. New types: `RuntimeKind` enum (`AnthropicApi`, `OpenAiApi`, `OpenAiCompatible`, `ClaudeCode`, `Codex`, `Opencode`) and `SessionMode` enum (`Native`, `Wrapped`, `Attended`). New function `validate_runtime_mode_compat(runtime: RuntimeKind, mode: SessionMode) -> Result<(), AppError>`. Allowed pairs: HTTP kinds only with `native`; CLI kinds with `wrapped` (and `attended` once Phase 11 lands; rejected until then with a clear error referencing the deferred feature). Mismatches return `AppError::Validation` with a `code: "runtime_mode_incompatible"` payload listing the runtime, the mode, and the modes the runtime supports. Session creation (`POST /api/workspaces/:wid/sessions`), kanban auto-spawn (`try_automate_lane`), and A2A `POST /api/a2a/messages` all call this. UI is updated separately in feat-053 to surface the error.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_runtime_mode_compat_anthropic_native_ok && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_runtime_mode_compat_anthropic_wrapped_rejected && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_runtime_mode_compat_claude_code_wrapped_ok && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_runtime_mode_compat_claude_code_native_rejected && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_runtime_mode_compat_attended_rejected_for_now && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_kanban_autospawn_rejects_incompatible_pair && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_a2a_rejects_incompatible_pair

features.behavior: Extend the `CodingAgent` trait so CLI-backed turns can carry per-session execution context. New `TurnContext` struct: `{ session_id, workspace_id, cwd, codebase_root, cli_resume_id: Option<String>, effective_permissions: PermissionSnapshot, cancellation_token: CancellationToken }`. `send_message` gains a `&TurnContext` parameter alongside `MessageRequest`; the signature change is breaking for any external implementer, but no external implementer exists (private trait). `AnthropicAgent` (native mode) ignores CLI-only fields; `AnthropicAgent` still receives cwd if it needs it for permission gating (it does not today, but the field is forwarded for future use). New `CliCodingAgent` is added in feat-043 onward and uses the full TurnContext. `SessionService::send_prompt` builds the TurnContext from session state and threads it through; the `done` SSE event includes the chosen runtime_kind + mode. `PermissionSnapshot` is a placeholder struct in this feature; the real mapper is feat-046.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_turn_context_construction && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_turn_context_passes_cwd_and_codebase && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_anthropic_agent_signature_change_compiles && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_session_service_passes_turn_context

features.behavior: Per-Runtime-Tool model cache. New `ModelCache` keyed on Provider id with a configurable TTL (default 5 minutes, much longer than the 10s provider-health TTL). HTTP `list_models` keeps its current behavior (cache hit returns the cached list). CLI `list_models` shells out to the registered binary; results are cached. The cache is invalidated on `add_provider` / `remove_provider` and on explicit `POST /api/providers/:id/refresh-models` (which also rejects the call for HTTP kinds to keep the API symmetric). Cache miss path: shell out, parse, store, return. Cache hit path: return the snapshot. `GET /api/providers/:id/models` is the public surface. Cache contents are not persisted; they are rebuilt at startup. The 10s health-check TTL from feat-033 is unchanged.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_model_cache_hit && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_model_cache_miss_shells_out && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_model_cache_invalidation_on_add_remove && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_model_cache_refresh_endpoint && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_model_cache_refresh_rejected_for_http && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_model_cache_ttl_expiry

features.behavior: Per-turn CLI subprocess runner used by every `CliCodingAgent`. New `CliRunner` in `crates/weave-server/src/agent/cli_runner.rs`. Takes a `CliInvocation { binary, args, env, cwd, stdin_prompt }` plus a `TurnContext` (for cancellation, session id tracking, and the active permission snapshot). Spawns via `tokio::process::Command`, captures stdout as a line stream (one JSON event per line for Claude Code's `stream-json` grammar; the line-stream is the parser's input, not the runner's concern), captures stderr to a bounded buffer (256KB, then truncated with a clear warning), waits on the child with `tokio::select!` on the cancel token, and on cancel sends SIGTERM to the child and SIGKILL after 5s. On exit, the child handle is removed from the per-session process table. The runner is intentionally per-turn: there is no long-lived process state and the child is reaped before the next turn starts. Exit code 0 is success; non-zero is mapped to `StreamEvent::Error` and the runner's stream is closed. Reuses `shell_exec`'s output truncation policy (100KB on stdout, last-256KB on stderr).
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_cli_runner_basic && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_cli_runner_cwd_env_args && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_cli_runner_cancel_sends_sigterm && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_cli_runner_exit_nonzero_maps_to_error && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_cli_runner_stderr_capture && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_cli_runner_per_turn_process_table

features.behavior: A test-only fake CLI binary under `crates/weave-server/tests/fakes/fake_cli/` that emulates a Claude-Code-style CLI for conformance tests. Reads `stream-json` style prompts from stdin (or argv, both supported via env var `FAKE_CLI_INPUT_MODE=stdin|argv`), emits a deterministic sequence of events on stdout (one JSON object per line) scripted by env vars (`FAKE_CLI_SCRIPT=text+tool+done`, etc.), supports `permission_denied`, `crash`, and `resume_unknown_session` scenarios via env-var flags, and echoes back the resume id (from argv or stdin) so tests can assert the runner passed it. The fake is a separate `[[bin]]` target in `weave-server` and is built by `cargo test -p weave-server`. Used by feat-051 (Claude Code e2e) and later by feat-058/059 (Codex, OpenCode).
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_fake_cli_emits_text_delta && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_fake_cli_emits_tool_use_and_result && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_fake_cli_permission_denied_scenario && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_fake_cli_crash_scenario && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_fake_cli_resume_unknown_session && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_fake_cli_echoes_resume_id

features.behavior: A line-stream parser that consumes Claude Code's `stream-json` output and converts each line into a `StreamEvent`. Handles: `text_delta` -> `TextDelta`; `tool_use` start (with `name` and empty `input`) -> `ToolUseStart`; `input_json_delta` -> `ToolUseDelta`; `tool_result` (with `tool_use_id` and `content`) -> `ToolResult`; `thinking` -> `Thinking`; `error` -> `Error`; `session_id` -> captured into `TurnContext::cli_resume_id` (so feat-047 can persist it); `done` (with `stop_reason`) -> `Done`. The parser is a state machine that tracks in-flight `content_block`s by their CLI-assigned ids and reconciles them with the Weave `tool_use` id. Malformed lines (invalid JSON, unknown event types, partial blocks) are logged at WARN and skipped — never fatal. The parser does NOT execute tools; that is the journey translator's job in feat-048. The parser is the only Claude-Code-specific piece; the rest of the runtime is shared with Codex and OpenCode via feat-057.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_claude_code_parser_text_delta && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_claude_code_parser_tool_use_start_delta && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_claude_code_parser_tool_result && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_claude_code_parser_thinking && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_claude_code_parser_session_id_capture && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_claude_code_parser_done_stop_reason && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_claude_code_parser_malformed_line_skipped

features.behavior: `PermissionMapper` trait + Claude Code implementation. Trait: `fn effective_permissions(kind: RuntimeKind, profile: ToolProfile) -> PermissionSnapshot`. Snapshot is a struct of CLI-specific flags the runner must build into argv / env. Claude Code mapper: takes the Weave `ToolProfile` (`full` | `implementation` | `review` | `planning` | `reporting`) and emits the corresponding Claude Code `--permission-mode` value, an allowlist of Weave tool names, and the CLI flags the user agreed to. Concrete mapping table lives in `crates/weave-server/src/agent/permissions/claude_code.rs`. The snapshot is passed through `TurnContext::effective_permissions` (feat-041) and consumed by the runner when building argv (feat-043). Review profile maps to `plan`; implementation maps to `accept-edits`; reporting maps to `default` with no shell. Mapping is intentionally minimal: it does NOT try to mirror Weave's exact tool allowlist into Claude Code's tool list — Claude Code's tool surface is the CLI's responsibility. A snapshot is also a struct that can serialize to JSON for debugging (logged at DEBUG on each turn).
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_permission_mapper_trait_compiles && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_permission_mapper_claude_code_full && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_permission_mapper_claude_code_implementation && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_permission_mapper_claude_code_review && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_permission_mapper_claude_code_planning && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_permission_mapper_claude_code_reporting && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_permission_snapshot_serializes_to_json

features.behavior: CLI-native resume metadata persistence with message-history replay fallback. On a successful CLI turn, the CLI's native session id (captured by the parser in feat-045) is stored in `sessions.runtime_metadata_json['cli_resume_id']`. On the next `send_message` for the same session, the runner reads the stored id and passes it to the CLI via the resume flag the CLI expects (e.g., `--resume <id>` for Claude Code). If the CLI rejects the resume id (non-zero exit, or a structured `resume_unknown_session` error in stdout / stderr), the runner clears the stored id and falls back to message-history replay: rebuild the assistant context from the stored messages table and re-invoke the CLI without the resume flag. The `done` SSE event includes a `resume_state: ` `native` | `replayed` | `none` `` so the UI can surface which mode was used. If the user changes `runtime_kind` on a new session, the prior `cli_resume_id` is NOT inherited. The session header pill (feat-054) shows the resume state.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_cli_resume_id_persisted_after_turn && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_cli_resume_id_passed_on_next_turn && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_cli_resume_fallback_replay_on_unknown && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_cli_resume_cleared_after_replay && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_cli_resume_not_inherited_across_runtime_switch && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_cli_resume_state_in_sse_done

features.behavior: `JourneyTranslator` that maps parsed CLI stream events into Weave trace events and `StreamEvent`s WITHOUT re-executing CLI tools. Text deltas pass through as `TextDelta`. Tool calls from the CLI (text + input from `ToolUseStart` + `ToolUseDelta`) become a `tool_call` trace event with the CLI's reported input and a placeholder duration captured from the time between `ToolUseStart` and the matching `ToolResult`; the trace is recorded in the `traces` table; `StreamEvent` is `ToolUseStart` / `ToolUseDelta` for UI display. The CLI is the source of truth for the tool result — the translator does NOT re-run the tool. File changes announced by the CLI (via a CLI-specific event type emitted by Claude Code) are recorded in the `file_changes` table. Thinking blocks become `decision` trace events. Errors become `error` trace events. The journey sidebar endpoint (feat-022) is unchanged. If a tool_use does not get a matching tool_result within the turn (CLI dropped it), the translator records a `tool_call` event with `status='orphaned'` so the journey still shows the attempt.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_journey_translator_text_passthrough && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_journey_translator_tool_call_recorded && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_journey_translator_tool_not_re_executed && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_journey_translator_file_change_recorded && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_journey_translator_thinking_to_decision && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_journey_translator_error_to_error && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_journey_translator_orphaned_tool_use && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_journey_translator_dedupes_file_changes

features.behavior: Child-process reaping on crash + per-session process tracking. Extends the existing `ActiveSessions` (added in feat-034) with a parallel `ActiveChildProcesses` table: `session_id -> tokio::process::Child handle + Pid`. New `service::startup::reap_cli_processes` (separate from `reap_orphans` for sessions) scans the filesystem for surviving CLI binaries whose parent pid is the Weave pid and whose session id matches a session that is no longer active (DB row terminal or missing) — terminates them with SIGTERM, waits 5s, then SIGKILL. Cancel routes SIGTERM to the tracked process and removes the entry on exit. Database-only orphan reaping (feat-034) is no longer sufficient once wrapped CLIs exist. Reaping is idempotent and re-runnable. Logs a count of reaped processes. Does not scan processes unrelated to Weave (filters on `parent_pid == weve_pid` and `argv[0]` matching a known registered CLI binary).
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_cli_reap_orphan_processes_terminates && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_cli_cancel_kills_tracked_process && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_cli_reap_idempotent && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_cli_reap_unrelated_process_untouched && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_cli_reap_runs_in_startup_sequence

features.behavior: Workspace-scoped CLI session validation. Wrapped sessions (any `mode=wrapped` + any CLI runtime_kind) MUST have `cwd` inside a registered `Codebase` row for the workspace (the existing `codebases.path` unique on `(workspace_id, path)` index is the lookup key — use canonical path comparison via `dunce::canonicalize` to handle Windows path quirks even though Weave is Linux-first). Reject at session creation with `AppError::Validation` and `code: "cwd_outside_codebase"` listing the cwd and the registered codebases for the workspace. Native (HTTP) sessions keep their existing behavior (cwd is optional and unconstrained). Kanban auto-spawn calls the same validator before creating a wrapped session. The provider's default working directory (if the provider row has one) is used as a fallback cwd inside the first matching codebase. A2A `POST /api/a2a/messages` also enforces the check.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_wrapped_session_requires_codebase && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_wrapped_session_cwd_outside_codebase_rejected && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_native_session_no_codebase_requirement && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_kanban_wrapped_autospawn_validates_cwd && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_a2a_wrapped_session_validates_cwd

features.behavior: Wire the first CLI Runtime Tool end-to-end. `ClaudeCodeCodingAgent` implements `CodingAgent` (with the `TurnContext` signature from feat-041), uses `CliRunner` (feat-043), the Claude Code stream parser (feat-045), the Claude Code `PermissionMapper` (feat-046), the journey translator (feat-048), and persists resume metadata (feat-047). `add_provider` for a `kind=cli` row whose `binary_path` matches a registered `claude-code` adapter builds a `ClaudeCodeCodingAgent`; matching is by binary basename for now. End-to-end conformance test uses the fake CLI harness (feat-044) as the registered binary: create a wrapped session, send a prompt, assert events stream through the SSE endpoint, assert resume state is `none` on the first turn and `native` on the second, cancel mid-stream and assert the child process is reaped, run a real smoke test with the actual `claude` binary as a manual check. Anthropic API in native mode stays green: every step in Phase 8 runs the full Rust test suite and `./init.sh` and the native tests must pass.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_claude_code_wrapped_session_create && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_claude_code_wrapped_streams_via_sse && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_claude_code_wrapped_resume_first_turn_native_second && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_claude_code_wrapped_cancel_mid_stream && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_claude_code_wrapped_falls_back_to_replay && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_claude_code_wrapped_records_journey && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_native_anthropic_still_passes_through_loop

features.behavior: Settings page replaces its hardcoded `type: 'anthropic'` form with a kind-aware form. The provider list renders a `kind` badge (HTTP / CLI) on each row. The 'Add provider' modal renders a kind picker first; once chosen, the rest of the form is the variant-specific shape. HTTP form fields: name, base_url, api_key (password input), default_model (free text + datalist of cached models). CLI form fields: name, binary_path (path picker that resolves to an absolute path), args (array of strings), env (map of string->string), default_model (free text + datalist of cached models), permission_mode (preset dropdown). Backend endpoint extension is minimal: `POST /api/providers` already accepts `kind` after feat-039. Existing providers (HTTP, kind='http') continue to render as today. Frontend tests cover both flows; the lint gate (Prettier, ESLint, tsc) is green.
features.verification: cd web && bun run test -- --run settings-runtime-tool && cd web && bun run lint && cd web && bun run typecheck

features.behavior: Replace the current 3-field 'New Session' modal with a 4-step wizard. Step 1: Runtime Tool picker. List comes from `GET /api/providers` (kind + display_name); only providers with healthy `health_check` results from the last cache window are selectable. Step 2: Role (specialist) picker. List comes from the existing specialists endpoint; the picker is filtered to specialists whose `tool_profile` is compatible with the chosen Runtime Tool (the matrix from feat-040 is enforced client-side for UX and server-side for safety). Step 3: Model picker. List comes from `GET /api/providers/:id/models` (the cache from feat-042); first entry is pre-selected. Step 4: workspace target (default: current workspace), optional codebase (filtered to the workspace's codebases), optional kanban task (filtered to active tasks not currently being worked on). Each step has a Back button; the final Submit button creates the session. The backend `POST /api/workspaces/:wid/sessions` is extended to accept `runtime_kind` and `mode` (defaulting to `claude-code`/`wrapped` if the chosen provider is CLI, `anthropic-api`/`native` if HTTP). Validation errors surface inline with the failing step highlighted.
features.verification: cd web && bun run test -- --run new-session-wizard && cd web && bun run test && cd web && bun run lint

features.behavior: The session page renders one of three layouts from the same `/session/:id` URL, chosen by the session's `mode`. `native` layout: existing chat view (unchanged for HTTP-backed sessions; used for Anthropic API and OpenAI API). `wrapped` layout: same chat view body, but `SessionHeader` gains a pill row showing the Runtime Tool display name, the active permission mode (from the most recent `PermissionSnapshot`), and the resume state (none / native / replayed). A small `WrappedSessionBanner` is shown above the message list on the first turn to surface the new interaction model in plain language. `attended` layout: not yet implemented (rejected at session creation until Phase 11); the layout slot is reserved. The wrapped and native layouts share 95% of components; the differences are isolated to `SessionHeader` and `WrappedSessionBanner`. The 'jump to latest' pill and SSE streaming behavior from feat-036 are unchanged.
features.verification: cd web && bun run test -- --run session-layouts && cd web && bun run test && cd web && bun run lint

features.behavior: Kanban column binding for `(runtime_kind, specialist_id)`. Add a nullable `runtime_kind` column to `columns` (default NULL = inherit workspace default). Migration is additive. `POST /api/boards/:bid/columns` and `PATCH /api/columns/:cid` accept `runtime_kind`. When a card is auto-spawned by lane automation, the created session uses the column's `(runtime_kind, specialist_id)` binding. If `runtime_kind` is NULL, behavior is unchanged (uses the workspace's default Runtime Tool). The frontend `ColumnHeader` shows a small Runtime Tool badge when `runtime_kind` is set and `auto_trigger` is on. The `try_automate_lane` service is updated to honor the binding. This feature also removes the silent first-provider fallback in the kanban flow: a column with no workspace-default Runtime Tool fails the auto-spawn with a clear error pointing at the column's binding. The A2A counterpart is feat-056.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_columns_migration_adds_runtime_kind && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_kanban_autospawn_uses_column_runtime_kind && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_kanban_autospawn_inherits_when_null && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_kanban_autospawn_errors_when_no_default && cd web && bun run test -- --run kanban-runtime-badge

features.behavior: A2A accepts an explicit `runtime_kind` and removes the silent first-provider fallback. `POST /api/a2a/messages` accepts an optional `runtime_kind` field on the request body. Resolution order: (1) request body's `runtime_kind` if present and the user is permitted to use it; (2) the session's existing `runtime_kind` if resuming; (3) an explicit configured default (new env var `WEAVE_A2A_DEFAULT_RUNTIME_KIND`, default `anthropic-api`). The current behavior of falling back to 'the first provider in the list' is removed. Sessions created via A2A get a `provider_id` resolved from the chosen `runtime_kind` via the provider registry; if no healthy provider matches, the call fails with `code: 'no_provider_for_runtime'`. The Agent Card at `GET /.well-known/agent.json` lists the configured default. Document the breaking change in CHANGELOG and the A2A section of `docs/api-contracts.md`.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_a2a_explicit_runtime_kind && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_a2a_uses_session_runtime_when_resuming && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_a2a_uses_configured_default && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_a2a_no_first_provider_fallback && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_a2a_errors_when_no_provider_for_runtime && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_a2a_agent_card_lists_default

features.behavior: A shared CLI adapter conformance test suite that every `CliCodingAgent` impl must pass before shipping. The suite lives in `crates/weave-server/tests/cli_conformance.rs` and exercises the adapter contract independent of which CLI is being wrapped. Conformance cases: (a) argv construction from a `CliInvocation` matches the recorded expected argv; (b) the stream parser maps a canonical event sequence to the expected `StreamEvent` sequence; (c) the resume id is captured and persisted; (d) the `PermissionMapper` produces a `PermissionSnapshot` whose serialized form matches the expected JSON for each `ToolProfile`; (e) the journey translator records the canonical tool_call and file_change events; (f) the runner handles `permission_denied` and `crash` scenarios from the fake CLI harness (feat-044) by mapping them to `StreamEvent::Error` and terminating the stream; (g) the adapter rejects sessions whose `cwd` is outside a registered codebase (the validation is enforced upstream in feat-050; the conformance test confirms the adapter's `TurnContext` plumbing is correct). Codex (feat-058) and OpenCode (feat-059) must pass the full suite. Real CLIs are not required for the suite — the fake harness is the conformance target.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server --test cli_conformance && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_conformance_argv_construction && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_conformance_stream_parser && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_conformance_resume_metadata && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_conformance_permission_mapper && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_conformance_journey_translator && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_conformance_error_scenarios && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_conformance_workspace_scoped_cwd

features.behavior: Concrete `CodexCodingAgent` (target Runtime Tool: `codex`). Implements `CodingAgent` with the `TurnContext` signature. Reuses `CliRunner` (feat-043), the conformance suite (feat-057), and the journey translator (feat-048). Codex-specific bits: argv shape (Codex CLI flags), the Codex-specific stream parser (a sibling of the Claude Code parser in feat-045), the Codex-specific `PermissionMapper` impl, and the resume flag Codex uses. Default model override per §8 of the strategy: if a Codex provider row has a `default_model`, the runner uses it. Same architectural surface as `ClaudeCodeCodingAgent`. Adapter is registered by binary basename (`codex`). End-to-end test uses the fake CLI harness adapted to emit Codex-style events.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_codex_wrapped_session_create && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_codex_wrapped_streams_via_sse && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_codex_resume_cycle && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_codex_permission_mapper && cargo test -p weave-server --test cli_conformance

features.behavior: Concrete `OpenCodeCodingAgent` (target Runtime Tool: `opencode`). Implements `CodingAgent` with the `TurnContext` signature. Reuses `CliRunner`, the conformance suite, and the journey translator. OpenCode-specific bits: argv shape, OpenCode-specific stream parser, OpenCode-specific `PermissionMapper` impl, and the resume flag OpenCode uses. Per §8 of the strategy, OpenCode pointed at Anthropic is modeled as `opencode` with a per-Runtime Tool default model override on the provider row, NOT as a separate `opencode-anthropic` Runtime Tool. Adapter is registered by binary basename (`opencode`). End-to-end test uses the fake CLI harness adapted to emit OpenCode-style events. The smoke check confirms that `default_model` override on the provider row reaches the CLI argv.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_opencode_wrapped_session_create && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_opencode_wrapped_streams_via_sse && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_opencode_resume_cycle && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_opencode_permission_mapper && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_opencode_default_model_override_reaches_argv && cargo test -p weave-server --test cli_conformance

features.behavior: Attended mode: a separate `Terminal` trait (NOT a `CodingAgent` impl) representing user-driven, long-lived subprocess hosting. The `Terminal` trait: `spawn(session, binary, args, env, cwd) -> TerminalHandle`, `attach(session_id) -> TerminalStream` (a duplex stream the frontend can render in an xterm.js pane), `detach(session_id)`, `kill(session_id) -> Result`. Lifecycle: spawn on session start, attach on session page open, detach on page close, kill on session cancel / shutdown. Watches the filesystem (via the `notify` crate) and the CLI's trace events to feed the journey sidebar — does NOT parse CLI stream events for execution (that's the wrapped-mode translator's job). The session page layout switcher (feat-054) renders the `attended` layout from the same `/session/:id` URL. Phase 11: do not start until Phase 8 wrapped mode is stable. Conformance test uses the fake CLI harness running in a long-lived mode (the harness from feat-044 is extended to support a `serve` subcommand that emits events as they occur and reads stdin for the user's keystrokes).
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_terminal_trait_compiles && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_terminal_spawn_and_attach && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_terminal_detach_keeps_process && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_terminal_kill_terminates && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_terminal_filesystem_watcher_feeds_journey && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_terminal_workspaces_scoped_validation && cd web && bun run test -- --run terminal-pane && cd web && bun run test

features.behavior: `docs/user/sessions.md` instructs the user to 'from the `Sessions` list, click `+ New Session` in the page header', but `web/src/app/pages/sessions.tsx` has no such button. Add a per-workspace `+ New Session` action to the Sessions list page so the doc matches the UI. Implementation: (1) Extract the inline New Session modal + form state from `web/src/app/pages/workspace.tsx` (the `<Modal>` block at the bottom plus the form state, providers fetch, and `useCreateSession` mutation) into a reusable `NewSessionModal` component in `web/src/components/new-session-modal.tsx`. The component takes `{ workspaceId, open, onClose, onCreated?(session) }` as props and owns the Provider / Specialist / Model form fields and the `useCreateSession` mutation. (2) Refactor `workspace.tsx` to use the extracted component for the existing page-level `+ New Session` button; behavior unchanged. (3) In `sessions.tsx`, render the modal once per `WorkspaceSessions` block; add a small `+ New Session` button next to the workspace heading that opens the modal pre-bound to that workspace's id; on success, navigate to `ROUTES.session(session.id)`. (4) Empty-state copy in `sessions.tsx` ('Create a workspace first to start sessions') stays as is — a workspace with no sessions should still show the per-workspace section so the button is reachable, not a global empty state. (5) The Provider dropdown is populated from `useProviders()` (already used by `workspace.tsx`); reuse the same hook in the new component. Acceptance: navigating to `/sessions` shows a visible per-workspace `+ New Session` action; clicking it opens a modal with that workspace's context; submitting creates a session and lands on `/sessions/:id`; the `workspace.tsx` `+ New Session` button still works; tsc + frontend lint clean.
features.verification: cd web && bun run test && cd web && bun run lint && cd web && bun run typecheck
features.evidence: ./init.sh all 3 layers pass: 611 Rust tests + 88 frontend tests (5 new) all green, clippy/fmt/prettier/ESLint clean. New page test web/src/app/__tests__/sessions.test.tsx (5 cases) covers: no-workspaces empty state, per-workspace + New Session button visible when workspace has zero sessions, session rows + button coexist, click button opens the NewSessionModal, submit form calls api.sessions.create with the right payload and navigates to /sessions/:id. New component web/src/components/new-session-modal.tsx owns the form + useProviders + useSpecialists (specialist is now a dropdown, was free text) + useCreateSession mutation + inline role=alert error. workspace.tsx refactored to use the new component (page shrank 344 → 220 lines). docs/user/sessions.md updated to say 'next to the workspace name' and to describe the specialist as a dropdown. Implementation note: the spec said 'render the modal once per WorkspaceSessions block' but the implementation uses one shared modal at page level (matches boards/codebases precedent, avoids N mutation instances, TanStack Query dedupes the providers/specialists queries).

features.behavior: Wire the existing-but-unused `find_by_cwd_prefix` helper to sessions: a session may be **bound** to a registered codebase (git repo) at create time, and the binding becomes a runtime sandbox for the agent's FS tools. Implementation: (1) New migration `010_session_codebase_id.sql` adds `codebase_id TEXT REFERENCES codebases(id) ON DELETE SET NULL` to `sessions` plus an index. The FK is `SET NULL` so deleting a codebase unbinds the session but does not delete it. (2) `store/sessions.rs` `Session` struct gains `codebase_id: Option<String>`; all SELECT/INSERT queries, `map_row`, `create`, `create_tx` updated to round-trip the new column. (3) `api/sessions.rs` `CreateSessionRequest` gains `codebase_id: Option<String>`; the handler passes it through to the service. (4) `service/sessions.rs` `create_session` resolves `codebase_id` via `CodebaseStore::get_in_workspace` (rejects cross-workspace and unknown ids with `AppError::NotFound`); on success, the codebase's `path` is copied onto the session's `cwd` (the binding wins over any supplied `cwd`). (5) `service/sessions.rs` `run_prompt_task` sets `ToolContext.codebase_root = session.cwd` when the session is bound (and `.` when unbound); the FS tools consult this for containment. The runtime falls back to `session.cwd` as the containment root even when the bound codebase has been deleted (so the sandbox does not silently flip off). (6) `tools/fs/mod.rs` gains a new `validate_read_path` helper (sibling to `validate_write_path`) that enforces containment but skips the control-plane guard — reads of `CLAUDE.md`/`Cargo.toml` etc. are still allowed. `fs_read`, `fs_list`, and `fs_search` (explicit-path branch) all call it. (7) `tools/shell.rs` and `tools/git/mod.rs` `validate_git_cwd` call `validate_read_path` on the explicit-`cwd` form so `shell_exec { cwd: '/etc' }` and `git_log { cwd: '/etc' }` are rejected for bound sessions. (8) `tools/fs/list.rs` and `tools/fs/search.rs` walkers use `entry.file_type()` (not `path.is_dir()`) and skip symlinks entirely — this is the load-bearing piece of the sandbox: `ln -s /etc <repo>/etc_link` cannot be used to escape via `fs_list`/`fs_search`. (9) `store/codebases.rs` drops the `#[allow(dead_code)]` on `find_by_cwd_prefix` since it now has a production caller; the `delete` method's doc comment is updated to reflect the new FK behavior. (10) Frontend: `Session` interface gains `codebase_id: string | null`; `NewSessionModal` adds a `Codebase` dropdown populated by `useCodebases(workspaceId)` with a disabled empty-state + link to `/codebases` when no codebases are registered; on submit, `codebase_id` is sent in the create payload. (11) `app/pages/session.tsx` session header shows a monospace pill with the codebase basename (full path on hover) when `session.cwd` is set. (12) Docs: `docs/user/sessions.md` describes the new `Codebase` field and adds a 'How sessions use a codebase' section that explicitly states the sandbox applies to the cwd arg / fs_read / fs_list / fs_search / explicit-cwd form of shell+git, NOT to the shell command body itself (so a bound session is a permission boundary on cwd, not a jail on the shell). `docs/user/codebases.md` mirrors the same section and updates the 'I registered a codebase but my session does not see it' pitfall. (13) Out of scope: kanban auto-spawn still passes `codebase_id: None` (the `tasks` model has no codebase ref); resume does not auto-inherit parent's binding. Acceptance: `cargo test -p weave-server` and `cd web && bun run test` green, `./init.sh` runs the system smoke (`/api/health` + `curl / | grep 'id="root"'`); user can open `+ New Session`, pick a codebase, submit; the session page header shows the path pill; bound sessions enforce FS containment at the cwd-arg level and refuse to follow symlinks inside the repo.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server && cd web && bun run test && ./init.sh
features.evidence: Layers 1 (lint) + 2 (tests) pass via `just check`: 616 Rust tests (5 new) + 90 frontend tests (2 new) green, clippy/fmt/prettier/ESLint clean. Layer 3 (system smoke + agent-browser validation) is the user's manual step — the verification command in the spec runs `./init.sh` and the user has signaled they'll drive the agent-browser smoke themselves; PROGRESS.md 'feat-062' section tracks the manual-validation checklist. New store tests: test_session_with_codebase_id (round-trips the new column), test_session_codebase_delete_sets_null (ON DELETE SET NULL keeps the row, drops the binding). New service tests: test_create_session_with_codebase_id_copies_path_to_cwd (binding wins over any supplied cwd), test_create_session_invalid_codebase_id_rejected (unknown id → AppError::NotFound), test_create_session_cross_workspace_codebase_rejected (ws-A codebase rejected for ws-B session). The legacy test suite (200+ fs/git/shell tests) stays green because containment is opt-in (only bound sessions are contained). New frontend tests in web/src/app/__tests__/sessions.test.tsx: empty-codebases disabled dropdown + /codebases link, and submit-with-codebase sends codebase_id in the create payload. Migration 010 is additive (1 column + 1 index); existing rows keep codebase_id = NULL. docs/user/sessions.md has a 'How sessions use a codebase' section that explicitly states the dual claim (cwd-arg containment yes, shell-body jail no); docs/user/codebases.md mirrors it. The simplify review pass: the runtime `codebase_root` resolution in run_prompt_task collapsed from 47 lines (with a SQL `find_by_cwd_prefix` call) to 3 lines (cwd when bound, . when unbound), because the runtime was recovering information already on the session row. The security-review pass: fixed a real symlink-escape bug in the fs_list/fs_search walkers (was using `path.is_dir()` which follows symlinks, now uses `entry.file_type()` and skips symlinks entirely); rewrote the user docs to make the dual claim explicit (the sandbox is on the cwd arg, not the shell command body).
